name: Mandarin Chicken Crepes
recipeIngredient: 1 1/4 lb Boneless chicken breasts, cut into 1/2 inch pieces
2 tbsp Firm packed light brown sugar
1 can (11-oz) mandarin oranges, drained and juice reserved
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine flour and salt.
2. In a small dish, whisk together eggs, milk, and 1 tablespoon of oil; pour into flour mixture.
3. In a 6-inch skillet or crepe pan, heat the remaining 1/4 teaspoon oil over medium heat.
4. Pour 2 tablespoons batter into the pan; swirl into a thin layer. Cook until the bottom is golden brown, then flip and cook the second side for 10 seconds. Repeat with the remaining batter, layering crepes between wax paper; set aside.
5. In a large skillet over high heat, saute chicken in the remaining 1 teaspoon oil until browned; remove to a bowl. Reduce heat to medium; saute onion, garlic, and ginger root until soft; remove garlic.
6. In a large dish, combine cornstarch, 2 tablespoons water, the brown sugar, vinegar, soy sauce, and pepper. Add water to mandarin juice to equal 1 cup; stir into the cornstarch mixture and add to skillet. Reduce heat to low; simmer, uncovered, for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
7. Set aside 1/4 cup sauce. Reserve a few oranges.
8. Add chicken, water chestnuts, and remaining oranges to skillet; simmer, uncovered, for 3 minutes.
9. To fill crepes, place 1/2 cup filling in the center of each crepe; fold edges over the middle, using sauce to seal. Spoon a small amount of sauce and oranges over each crepe; sprinkle with parsley.
